name: Runtime Task Sandbox Split
description: A delivery workflow that proves runtime-owned planning and release-summary work while repository mutation and artifact upload stay in task sandboxes.
outcome: The seeded repository gains a deterministic status-report command, the implementation summary is uploaded as an artifact, and the release summary is completed without turning planning or release-summary work into task-sandbox execution.

definition.process_instructions:
Run the workflow through the authored stages `planning`, `implementation`, and `release-summary`. Treat each stage as a distinct checkpoint of evidence and handoff quality: `planning` exists to Implementation-ready guidance exists in memory and handoff form; `implementation` exists to The repository change, verification proof, and uploaded artifact exist; `release-summary` exists to The uploaded implementation summary is reviewed and the final release summary is recorded.
Use these roles deliberately: `runtime-split-planning-analyst` Plans the smallest safe change without using repository tools; `runtime-split-implementation-engineer` Implements the repository change, verifies it, and uploads the implementation artifact; `runtime-split-release-coordinator` Reads the uploaded implementation artifact and records the final release summary without repository tools.
Happy path: start with the earliest unfinished stage, dispatch the best matching producing role for that stage, require a full handoff with concrete artifacts or evidence, then move to the next stage only when the current output is usable. When a later stage is primarily evaluative, dispatch the reviewer or assessor roles after the producing role has handed off; when a later stage is packaging or release-oriented, dispatch the coordinating role only after upstream evidence is complete.
Unhappy path: If any role reports missing evidence, partial delivery, request changes, rejection, blocking risk, or an escalation, keep the current subject open, route work back to the most relevant producing role, and require a fresh handoff before trying again. If two decisions conflict, preserve both in history, but route using the newest subject state and the most restrictive still-active finding. Do not skip a stage just because a later role believes it can improvise around incomplete upstream work. This playbook intentionally separates runtime-visible orchestration from task-sandbox execution. `runtime-split-planning-analyst` and `runtime-split-release-coordinator` should work from runtime-visible context and uploaded artifacts, while `runtime-split-implementation-engineer` performs the repository work in the task sandbox.
Completion rules: finish only after the last required stage has a final, internally consistent handoff, the final package matches the current subject state, and every role named above has either contributed or been intentionally skipped for a clear reason grounded in the playbook. Do not fabricate approvals, assessments, escalations, or branch changes that the playbook does not actually justify, and do not treat stale findings as permission to advance new work.

parameters.name: goal
parameters.description: What the seeded repository change must accomplish

parameters.name: scenario_name
parameters.description: Stable identifier that the new status-report output and uploaded summary must mention

columns.label: Planned
columns.description: Work is defined but not yet assigned.

columns.label: Blocked
columns.description: Work is blocked and needs intervention.

columns.label: Done
columns.description: The work item meets its acceptance criteria.

stages.name: planning
stages.goal: Implementation-ready guidance exists in memory and handoff form.

stages.name: implementation
stages.goal: The repository change, verification proof, and uploaded artifact exist.

stages.name: release-summary
stages.goal: The uploaded implementation summary is reviewed and the final release summary is recorded.
